back_translation: Remind them to submit to rulers and those who have authority, to be obedient, and to be ready for every good deed
Not to speak evil of anyone, to be non-quarrelsome, gentle, showing full meekness toward everyone
For once, we ourselves were also foolish, disobedient, misled, slaves of various desires and pleasures, living in malice and envy, being hated and hating one another
But when the kindness and the love that God our Savior had toward mankind had appeared
He saved us, not because of the righteous deeds we had done, but according to His mercy, through the washing of new birth, and the renewal by the Holy Spirit
Whom He poured out abundantly upon us, through Jesus Christ our Savior
So that when we have been declared righteous by His grace, we may become heirs according to the hope of eternal life

This saying is trustworthy, and I want you to affirm these things resolutely, so that those who have believed in God will give careful attention to devoting themselves to doing good. These things are good and profitable for people
But avoid foolish controversies, genealogies, strife, and arguments about the Law, because these things are unprofitable and worthless
Reject a person who causes division, after warning him a first and second time
Knowing that such a person has turned aside to the wrong path and is sinning, being self-condemned
When I send Artemas or Tychicus to you, make every effort to come to me at the city of Nicopolis, because I have decided to spend the winter there
Do your best with all effort to send Zenas the Law-expert and Apollos on their way, so that they will lack nothing
And let our people learn to devote themselves to doing good, in order to meet urgent needs, so that they will not be unfruitful
All who are with me send their greetings to you. Greet those who love us in the faith. May grace abide with all of you
